title: Fungal nail infection
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports yellow, white, or brown discoloration, thickening, brittleness, splitting, debris, roughness, pain, and clinician-obtained clipping or sample for microscopy or culture.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports colour, thickness, brittleness, debris, lifting, pain, and clinician-obtained nail sampling.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports yellow, white, or brown discoloration, thickening, brittleness, splitting, debris, roughness, pain, and clinician-obtained clipping or sample for microscopy or culture.

exact_section.heading: What are the symptoms and how is diagnosis made?

assessment.text: Supports onychomycosis and fungal-nail terminology plus affected-nail, spread, trauma, and host-risk context.
assessment.rationale: Supports fungal-nail identity, affected nail, spread, trauma, and host-risk context.

source_limitations: Cellulitis, systemic symptoms, immunocompromise escalation, photographs, nail-care or hygiene advice, fixed follow-up, medicines, liver tests, and treatment remain unsupported.
Qualified UAE clinician review is required.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
